THE JUNGLE
From City in the Jungle 
To Jungle in the City

Comprehensive Analysis
The satellite cities in Phnom Penh are normally 
the consequences of the investment  private 
sector resulting in what  the  other  researches 
called "inaccessible green spaces".
Koh Pich is an expansion of Phnom Penh as a
satellite city in which the prevalent interest is
related to the margin of profit of a company.
At present, the area is fragmented by roads.
However, there is sufficient green to potentially
be connected a network within the island.
To become the central hub. the City Hall area
would need to be connected to the Fair, the
Elysee and the rest of the island.
The analysis envisioned not only the
green
site, but also the road structure connecting
the green network to existing buildings.

Concept Diagram
Site
The site is located in portion of the area where the green
elements are fragmented and enclosed.
Subtraction
The
corners
are
being
subtracted
to
make
welcoming
connections to the public rather than having a solid and formal
object opposing to the street.
Extrusion
The mass is split and extruded to create variation of heights
connecting the ground level up to the height of the city hall.
De-massing & Characterization
The visual impact of the building on the site is further reduced
by treating the floors of the building as superimposed layers
and avoiding the use of vertical fa9ade windows.
Offset
Then it is offset to reduce to perception of having a massive
object in the context. In addition, the green terraces are 
introduced on top of the building.
Contextualization
The landscape offers a seamless uninterrupted  experience
connecting the shophouses, to the Town Hall, then  through 
the building to the other side and along the main road to the

Masterplan
The city hall is located in an isolated area that is
disproportionate to its surroundings and isolated by
a
fence.
However,
there
is
adequate
green
infrastructure
and
potential
to
create
a
green
corridor towards the river, thus forming a network of
green spaces across the island.
The building will offer rooftop spaces and a bridge
connecting to Treelion Park, the river, and other
nearby buildings. This will establish the building as a
central
hub
on
the
island,
providing
accessible
public green areas and convenient pathways.
The building is designed to attract young people and
is activated through its various functions , such as
an open market, vendors, shops, anchor shops,
sports, offices, cafe and bar,
which are integrated
into the green space and the parking lot. The
building itself would also allow for flexible spaces for
events
and
promotions.
The
diverse
range
of
activities
available to the public on site would
activate the
green space and add value to the
surrounding area.

City Hall

Ground Floor
On the ground level, from the main entrance, there are
small subdivisions of steps which are functioned as
vendors as to welcome, visitors. Passing
through
small terraces and vendors, the landscape
invites
the users into the area with a formal element at the
west wing of the city hall and the market at the south.
From
this
point
onwards,
the
spaces
are  
decentralized from the pavilion towards different
directions.
Directing
towards
southwest
is
a
connection to another site with a journey through
urban forests.
On the south side of the market, is the cafe with
seating places being ventilated,
shaded,
floated on
the water, giving the experience of submerging into
the water and inside a forest.
Another direction towards the east, going through the
urban forests, in a series of steps across the wedding
platform and to the other side of the city hall, offering
the sensation of terraces and jungle .

Arial View, Southeast Corner: The elevated car parks and shops are connected with stairs to the rooftop.Then the city hall's park introduces green canopies, connecting to the food street at the water channel.
Renderings

Renderings
Arial View, Southwest Corner : The green element is introduced and continues upwards with the sloping
garden, the pavilion, sunken gardens as well as green canopies and recreation area.
Landscape: The landscape can take on a linear form, which expresses horizontality through the 
progression of steps leading to green and blue spaces.
Open Office : Office spaces are integrated into the landscape, enclosing only private spaces. The use of
branching column, recessed back imposing only the horizontal slabs.
Gym : The emphasizes of natural element and horizontality of the spaces towards the interior. lntroducuing
also the function such as gym and library to compliment with the office.

Renderings
South Elevation: Garden Ramp, connecting to different levels of the terrace, along side the Sunken Garden and pavillions.
East Elevation: Superimposed Horizontal slabs, with recessed columns at the back, with Pavillions and Recreation spaces on the rooftops.

BIO-CITY
Restoration of the Cultural & 
Natural Connection in the City

Location
Bio-city is a project that attempts to solve the problem
of dense housing in Phnom Penh, Cambodia. In
exchange for the development project, a lake (Beoung
Kok) was filled to prepare for new high-rise building.
"Bio" refers to the life including the life of the natural
landscape, and the life of the people who lives as
part of nature as well. While "City" refers to the habitat
of the human being.
The memories of the nature, such as the lake and the
forest, and the identity of the people, lifestyle, culture,
especially with Cambodians where water was once
cherished by the civilization and has its whole culture
around the water. This has been lost, blurred with the
new idea of modernity and contemporary building.
60 rn
---.J
00
3

762m

N
C)
Site

Conceptual Form
Bio-city firstly respects the elements of nature that
once existed in the site and re-imagine it. The
feeling of the lake is re-created along its axis, the dug-
up soil is refilled as hills, creating a natural
landscape.
Then the chaotic arrangement from the unplanned
development at the north is respected, with its unique
pattern, extending from the surrounding shop houses
inwards the site, and re-envision the patterns and
houses in a new urbanized context.
The patterns on the ground takes the shape of the
neighboring urban fabric on top of the linear water
corridor for recollection.
The recreation spaces are arranged in the hills and
lakes naturally while the residential spaces are kept
free from the ground level.
Other
activities are also included to further enhance
the sensation of living in a traditional village that
once existed in the lake.

of the traditional houses.

Renderings
Green Corridor: Activating the ground level with green corridor, vendors, and other activities while the 
above floors remain as residential units.
Interior : Inviting the natural element to the space, recreate the family relationship in a traditional lifestyle 
while adapting to the urbanized context
Interior : Harmonious usage of traditional elements and the landscape that once existed around the site, 
including earth, water and wood.
Interior : Unifying vertical farming, natural landscape and Khmer lifestyle together to express modernized 
Khmer culture while still being sustainable.

Facade Details
Sliding
Pivoting
Facade Detail : The facade system allows each panel to be stationary, slided, or pivoted to maintain the vegetation. Variaion of facade panels also make it possible to create a constantly changing elevation in which 
each panel.

Exploration of various 
design approaches
OTHER
EXPERIMENTS

The 9 Paradise
Forest  : The sensation of a forest is brought through a big space supported by tree-inspired columns. The 
soul and logs of trees with low light settings, delivers the experience of being in a forest. 
Exhibition Space : A double-height space, supported by glasses and curved columns, viewing the rich 
cultural properties of patterns on the glass and its terrains outside, connecting the interior and exterior.
Cave : A small sitting area with lights mimicking the spatial sensation of a Cave, locating in a low height 
space, and directional light from above.
Design Process : The 9 Paradise was meant to be a competition project for Slovak Exhibition Expo in 
Japan, Showcasing the territory and culture of Slovakia.

Picasso’s Viewing Gallery
Top View : Picasso’s Viewing Gallery is an experiment of form following the concept of cubism. It takes 
3 painting of “The Accordionist” of Picasso and divided it from different perception of the Accordionist. 
Elevation 1 :The material is further adapted to show the difference of elements. This includes the use of 
Solid, Reflective, Transparent, and Semi-Transparent.
Elevation 2 : The structure is also adapted to the different material, making is as a solid-load bearing 
structure, frame, and tensile structure.
Elevation 3 : The resulted product is a statement of strong element in the center, tilted outwards, 
supported by light elements. As one walk through the space, they will experience the changes of light, 
shadow, reflection, solidity etc. as the property of material differs,
Elevation 1
Elevation 2
Elevation 3

Geometric, Organic Shape & Biomimicry
Composition of Geometric Shape: The spaces are arranged with different composition on each level, 
and staircase in the middle. A horizontal element is protruded, exaggerated to make the shape dramatic.
Adjusting the Geometric shape : The rectilinear shape of the levels are subtracted to mimic the shape of 
the crystal.
Formal Expression : The Crystal is further studied to give the form to the levels.
Opening and Enclosure : Panels are then added to create the opening and enclosed spaces, while 
keeping both the interior and exterior the same language of geometry..
